author: Dr. Lizard
title: What blast count ranges define MDS versus AML in TP53-mutated cases under ICC?
answer: ICC uses 0-9% for MDS, 10-19% for MDS/AML, and 20% or more for AML with mutated TP53.
detail: The 10-19% blast range creates a unique MDS/AML category specifically for TP53-mutated cases in ICC.
All three categories require TP53 VAF at least 10% for the mutated TP53 designation in ICC.
WHO-5 does not create a separate MDS/AML category for the 10-19% blast range.
At 20% or more blasts, ICC designates it as AML with mutated TP53 regardless of allelic status.
releaseNote: ICC uses specific blast count ranges to differentiate MDS, MDS/AML, and AML in TP53-mutated cases, with a unique category for the 10-19% range and a requirement of at least 10% TP53 VAF for mutated TP53 designation.
